prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "卷腹" (crunch) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a crunch (smaller range than sit-up): lying supine with both knees bent at 90 degrees and feet flat on the ground; only the upper back and shoulders lifted off the ground (about 30-40 degrees), with the lower back remaining in contact with the ground; hands placed lightly behind the head with elbows out, chin tucked; focused contraction of the upper rectus abdominis; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: rectus abdominis (especially the upper portion). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: obliques and transverse abdominis. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis (upper portion); "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "腹横肌" pointing to the transverse abdominis. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
